name: Queen of Chilis (Cincinnati-style), Kate Connally
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Barbeque sauce (I think I used K.C. Masterpiece)
recipeInstructions:
1. Chop 2 of the onions and set aside. Chop remaining onion fine.
2. Season a large skillet with salt.
3. Turn heat to medium and add meat, finely chopped onion, and garlic. Break up meat with fork and cook until it is browned.
4. Drain fat from the pan.
5. Add barbeque sauce and water. Bring mixture to a boil.
6. Stir in remaining seasonings: chili powder, black pepper, grated unsweetened chocolate, ground cumin, turmeric, allspice, cinnamon, cloves, coriander, cardamom, and salt.
7. Cover and simmer over very low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally and adding tomato juice as needed to prevent drying out.
8. The chili is best when allowed to age overnight in the refrigerator before reheating.
9. To serve, layer spaghetti on a plate (a small oval plate is traditional), top with hot chili, add a sparse layer of beans, then sprinkle chopped onions. Sprinkle cheese over the hot chili and serve immediately, accompanied by oyster crackers.
